title: libertia umbellata
intro: Libertia umbellata, also known as New Zealand iris or brodiea, is a perennial plant native to New Zealand, characterized by its striking white and yellow flowers. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating skin conditions such as eczema and acne, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Libertia umbellata include its ability to soothe and calm irritated skin, as well as its potential to reduce inflammation and promote wound healing. Libertia umbellata can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, and is often combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
